_comment: Curation overlay for gto-poker-trainer. Sets first_delivery_outcome based on run-report evidence.
first_delivery_outcome_rationale: Artifact works: CV verdict pass with PNV.1 verified end-to-end under jsdom production fidelity (10 hands → reload → 5 hands, alltime_hands transitioning 0 → 10 → 10 (post-reload) → 15, zero network calls). However, the run surfaced four architectural amendment candidates and one Sev 0 fix (the quoted 'raise_3.5' key caught by Integrator's node --check), plus charter-compliance gaps where Discovery's per-IP TaskCreate and TD's Researcher TaskCreate did not fire as the v1.8 charters specify. The artifact is delivered and functional, but the run-report's own framing surfaces enough mid-build remediation to warrant 'concerns' rather than a clean ship. No user re-prompt occurred.
notes: Source: runs/gto-poker-trainer/run-report.md §What worked, §What broke/surprised, §Architectural amendment candidates. No RCA exists for this run; no v16-reaudit. The PNV pass under production-fidelity jsdom is meaningful evidence that the artifact does what the prompt asked for at first contact, but the four amendment candidates the run-report itself raises are the 'concerns' qualifier.
